content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN. He defines the alternating sum of the array as:


S=∣A1∣−∣A2∣+∣A3∣−∣A4∣+…(−1)N−1⋅∣AN∣S = |A_1| - |A_2| + |A_3| - |A_4| + \ldots (-1)^{N-1}\cdot |A_N|S=∣A1​∣−∣A2​∣+∣A3​∣−∣A4​∣+…(−1)N−1⋅∣AN​∣
Chef is allowed to perform the following operation on the array **at most once**:


Choose two indices iii and jjj (1≤i<j≤N)(1 \leq i <  j \leq N)(1≤i<j≤N) and swap the elements AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​.
Find the **maximum** alternating sum Chef can achieve by performing the operation **at most once**.

**Note:** ∣X∣|X|∣X∣ denotes the absolute value of XXX. For example, ∣−4∣=4|-4| = 4∣−4∣=4 and ∣7∣=7|7| = 7∣7∣=7.


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - size of the array AAA.
Second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers - denoting the elements of array AAA.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line, the **maximum** alternating sum Chef can obtain by performing the operation **at most once**.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then for each test case:
- A line with an integer N, the size of the array.
- A line with N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible alternating sum after performing at most one swap.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: One optimal way is to perform no operations. Thus the alternating sum is ∣10∣−∣−10∣=10−10=0|10| - |-10| = 10 - 10 = 0∣10∣−∣−10∣=10−10=0.
Test Case 222: One optimal way is to choose i=2i = 2i=2 and j=5j = 5j=5. After swapping, the array is [−3,1,−1,0,−2,2,3][-3, 1, -1, 0, -2, 2, 3][−3,1,−1,0,−2,2,3]. The alternating sum in this case is ∣−3∣−∣1∣+∣−1∣−∣0∣+∣−2∣−∣2∣+∣3∣=6|-3| - |1| + |-1| - |0| + |-2| - |2| + |3| = 6∣−3∣−∣1∣+∣−1∣−∣0∣+∣−2∣−∣2∣+∣3∣=6.

solution.approach:
Let Bi = |Ai|. The alternating sum is S = Σ (-1)^{i-1} * Bi. Swapping two elements i and j only changes the sum if i and j have opposite parity, because the sign of both positions is the same otherwise. For an odd index (sign +1) and an even index (sign –1) the change caused by swapping is Δ = 2 * (Beven – Bodd). Therefore the best possible increase is 2 * max_{odd o, even e} (Beven – Bodd). This is achieved by taking the smallest B among odd positions and the largest B among even positions. If this difference is non‑positive we do not swap. The answer is original S plus max(0, 2 * (maxEven – minOdd)). The algorithm scans the array once, keeping:
- original alternating sum,
- the maximum B on even positions,
- the minimum B on odd positions.
All operations are O(N) per test case, O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, overall O(Σ N) ≤ 2·10^5.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().split()))
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it)
        orig = 0
        max_even = -1
        min_odd = 10**18
        for i in range(1, N+1):
            x = next(it)
            b = abs(x)
            if i & 1:  # odd index
                orig += b
                if b < min_odd:
                    min_odd = b
            else:      # even index
                orig -= b
                if b > max_even:
                    max_even = b
        diff = max_even - min_odd
        if diff > 0:
            orig += 2*diff
        out_lines.append(str(orig))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
